title: Method for identifying a job opportunity and profiling workers at a target location
abstract: Disclosed is a method for identifying a job opportunity suitable for a skilled worker, the method comprising generating worker preference in a target location based on skill set information and skilled worker information retrieved from one or more databases, the skill set information relating to at least one skill that skilled worker possesses and the skilled worker information comprising information relating to an availability of the skilled worker; calculating a worker preference score based on the generated worker preference; retrieving available job data from the one or more databases based on the calculated worker preference score, the job data identifying one or more job opportunities that are available at the target location; and identifying the job opportunity in the target location suitable for the skilled worker based on the calculated worker preference score.
claims:
Claims Claims A method for identifying a job opportunity suitable for a skilled worker, the method comprising: generating worker preference in a target location based on skill set information and skilled worker information retrieved from one or more databases, the skill set information relating to at least one skill that skilled worker possesses and the skilled worker information comprising information relating to an availability of the skilled worker; calculating a worker preference score based on the generated worker preference; retrieving available job data from the one or more databases based on the calculated worker preference score, the job data identifying one or more job opportunities that are available at the target location; and identifying the job opportunity in the target location suitable for the skilled worker based on the calculated worker preference score. The method as claimed in claim 1, wherein retrieving available job data from the one or more databases comprises only retrieving available job data that has a worker preference score higher than a predetermined threshold score. The method as claimed in claim 1 or 2 wherein retrieving available job data from the one or more databases comprises: retrieving employer information for the one or more job opportunities, each of the employer information comprises a job requirement of each of a plurality of employers; and wherein identifying the job opportunity in the target location further comprises matching the job requirement of each of the plurality of employers with the skill set information. The method as claimed in claim 2 or 3, wherein the skill set information comprises a compensation amount that the skilled worker would like to receive; 

 wherein the employer information comprises an expected amount for each of the one or more job opportunities; and and wherein identifying the job opportunity in the target location comprises corresponding the compensation amount and the expected amounts. 5. The method as claimed in any one of claims 2-4, wherein the employer information further comprises an industrial description of each of the plurality of employers. 6. The method as claimed in claim 5, wherein identifying the job opportunity in the at least one target location comprises matching the skill set information with the industry description in the at least one target location. 7. The method as claimed in any one of the preceding claims, further comprising the step of processing skill set information, the skill set information indicating skilled worker profile and training that the skilled worker has received. 8. The method as claimed in claim 6, wherein skilled worker information further comprises a verification message verifying the training that the skilled worker has received. 9. The method as claimed in claim 8, wherein the verification message identifies a number of hours that the skilled worker has worked, the number of hours being used to calculate an amount to pay the skilled worker. 10. The method as claimed in claim 8 or 9, wherein the verification message identifies at least a type of training that the skilled worker needs. 11. The method as claimed in any one of claims 8-10, further comprising linking a plurality of employers and a plurality of third parties using the verification message. 12. The method as claimed in any one of the preceding claims, wherein the at least one target location comprises at least one selected from a group consisting of a continent, a country, a state, a city and an area covered by a postal code. 13. The method as claimed in any one of the preceding claims, calculating a worker preference score based on the generated worker preference comprises the steps of: identifying a plurality of variables, the plurality of variables being dependent on any one of the skill set information and skilled worker information; assigning weights to the plurality of variables; and calculating the worker preference score based on the weights on the plurality of variables. 14. An apparatus for identifying a job opportunity suitable for a skilled worker, the apparatus comprising: at least one processor; and at least one memory including computer program code; the at least one memory and the computer program code configured to, with at least one processor, cause the apparatus at least to: generate worker preference in a target location based on skill set information and skilled worker information retrieved from one or more databases, the skill set information relating to at least one skill that skilled worker possesses and the skilled worker information comprising information relating to an availability of the skilled worker; calculate a worker preference score based on the generated worker preference; retrieve available job data from the one or more databases based on the calculated worker preference score, the job data identifying one or more job opportunities that are available at the target location; and identify the job opportunity in the target location suitable for the skilled worker based on the calculated worker preference score. 15. The apparatus according to claim 14, wherein the at least one memory and the computer program code is further configured with the at least one processor to only retrieve available job data that has a worker preference score higher than a predetermined threshold score. 16. The apparatus according to any one of claims 14-15, wherein the at least one memory and the computer program code is further configured with the at least one processor to: retrieve employer information for the one or more job opportunities, each of the employer information comprises a job requirement of each of the plurality of employers; and match the job requirement of each of the plurality of employers with the skilled worker information. 17. The apparatus according to any one of claims 14-16, wherein the employer information further comprises an industrial description of each of the plurality of employers. 18. The apparatus according to claim 17, wherein identifying the job opportunity in the at least one target location comprises matching the skill set information with the industry description in the at least one target location. 19. The apparatus according to any one of claims 14-18, wherein the at least one memory and the computer program code is further configured with the at least one processor to process skill set information, the skill set information indicating skilled worker profile and training that the skilled worker has received. 20. The apparatus according to claim 19, wherein skilled worker information further comprises a verification message verifying the training that the skilled worker has received. 21. The apparatus according to any one of claims 14-20, wherein the at least one target location comprises at least one selected from a group consisting of a continent, a country, a state, a city and an area covered by a postal code. 22. The apparatus according to any one of claims 14-21, wherein the at least one memory and the computer program code is further configured with the at least one processor to: identify a plurality of variables, the plurality of variables being dependent on any one of the skill set information and skilled worker information; assign weights to the plurality of variables; and calculate the worker preference score based on the weights on the plurality of variables
